name: Neapolitan Mushroom Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Fresh mushrooms, wiped clean, stems trimmed, and sliced
4 Fresh plum tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped OR 4 canned Italian plum tomatoes, drained and chopped
1 tbsp Minced fresh marjoram or 1 teaspoon dried marjoram
1 tbsp Minced fresh thyme or 1 teaspoon dried thyme
3 tbsp Chopped fresh parsley, plus about 1/4 cup chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the porcini mushrooms in 1/2 cup of warm water for about 30 minutes. Drain, reserving the liquid.
2. Rinse, dry, and chop the porcini mushrooms. Strain the soaking liquid through washed cheesecloth, paper towels, or a coffee filter. Set aside.
3. Melt the butter and olive oil in a large pot over low heat. Add the onion and garlic. Cook, stirring frequently, until the onion is wilted but not browned, about 5 minutes.
4. Add the porcini mushrooms and cook for 8 minutes. Increase the heat to medium and add the fresh mushrooms. Cook until the juices run, about 10 minutes. Add the reserved porcini soaking liquid and continue to cook until reduced by half, about 5 minutes. Add the tomatoes, salt, pepper, marjoram, and thyme; cook for 5 minutes.
5. Add the broth and simmer for 15 minutes.
6. While the soup is cooking, toast the bread in a 400°F oven until lightly browned on both sides. Remove and brush one side with the remaining olive oil. Use more oil if necessary.
7. In a small bowl, beat the egg yolks with the Parmesan cheese, pecorino romano cheese, and chopped parsley. Gradually whisk 1 cup of the hot soup into the egg yolks to warm them. Reverse and slowly whisk the egg yolk mixture into the soup. Cook, stirring constantly, over medium-low heat until the soup thickens.
8. Place a piece of toast in each of 8 bowls. Ladle in the hot soup and sprinkle with additional grated Parmesan cheese and chopped parsley.
Serves 8.
